{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        blocks: list[nn.Module] = []
        for stage in range(max(DEPTH, 1)):
            stage_channels = min(channels * (CHANNEL_MULT**stage), 256)
            if stage == 0:
                blocks.append(nn.Conv2d(channels, stage_channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias))
                if USE_BATCHNORM:
                    blocks.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(stage_channels))
                blocks.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
            else:
                blocks.append(nn.Conv2d(channels, stage_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias))
                if USE_BATCHNORM:
                    blocks.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(stage_channels))
                blocks.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
            channels = stage_channels
            blocks.append(ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM))
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*blocks)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Replace the underpowered one-block micro net with the stage-wise residual layout already used successfully in the other CIFAR-10 workloads, while bumping the micro capacity constants to match it.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching","summaries"],"rationale":"The schedule is already doing most of the work; the next low-complexity probe is to run the same fixed-step OneCycle setup with a slightly hotter peak learning rate and stronger label smoothing, which often helps small CIFAR-10 residual nets finish sharper in the same budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The fixed-step OneCycle recipe is still likely conservative; a modest increase in peak learning rate is the simplest remaining knob with a realistic chance of squeezing out a bit more validation loss without changing the architecture again.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"summaries","declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The short fixed-step run may be decaying too hard at the end; keep the same max LR but give the OneCycle schedule a longer high-LR phase and a less tiny floor so the last part of the budget still learns instead of coasting.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else max(1, steps_per_epoch * 100)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=0.15,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )","new":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else max(1, steps_per_epoch * 100)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=0.2,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=25.0,
    )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The run is likely past the point where more schedule aggressiveness helps much; the remaining gap is more likely from the loss being over-smoothed relative to the hard-label validation metric, so ease the smoothing back toward the earlier, less regularized setting.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last smoothing reduction was a regression, so restore the stronger smoothing that already produced the best result and then ease AdamW decay a bit; this keeps the confirmed-good optimization shape while reducing the remaining regularization pressure.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"summaries","declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The remaining gain is likely from spending more of the short fixed-step budget at useful learning rates instead of tailing off too aggressively, so keep the OneCycle peak but raise its floor for the final phase.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        final_div_factor=25.0,","new":"        final_div_factor=10.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Undo the too-aggressive schedule floor that just regressed the run, and give the pooled representation a slightly wider classifier head; the model still looks capacity-limited rather than overfit-limited.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        final_div_factor=10.0,","new":"        final_div_factor=25.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The wider classifier head did not help, and the best visible result came from the smaller head with the same optimizer/schedule shape, so revert that capacity increase and keep the current regularized training setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Try a larger per-step batch in the fixed-step regime; with the current OneCycle setup and no time pressure from the verifier, this is a low-complexity way to make each update less noisy and expose more data per step.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The bigger batch clearly helped, which suggests the optimization is still somewhat underfit; easing AdamW decay a bit is the safest remaining regularization tweak without adding compute or destabilizing the now-good training recipe.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The lower decay regressed the run, so restore the previously better regularization level before exploring anything more aggressive; the current setup was starting to look under-regularized rather than truly capacity-limited.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The strongest gain so far came from exposing more data per fixed step, so take a smaller additional step in that direction rather than changing the model again; this keeps the update rule simple while increasing sample coverage per iteration.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The larger batch has made the optimization much cleaner, so the remaining slack is more likely in the step size than the model shape; a slightly hotter OneCycle peak is the simplest way to keep progress moving without adding any runtime cost.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The larger batch has made the run stable enough to tolerate a slightly hotter peak step size, and the current gains have mostly come from better optimization rather than model changes, so keep that axis moving up a bit.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The larger-batch OneCycle run is still improving as the peak step size rises, so the remaining room is more likely in the learning-rate scale than in the model itself; push the peak a bit higher while keeping the same schedule shape.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The hotter peak overshot, so restore the better learning-rate scale and continue the strongest trend in the run by giving each fixed step a bit more data; the batch-size axis has been the main source of gains so far.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The 192 batch overshot the sweet spot, so move back toward the better batch-size neighborhood while keeping the high-LR OneCycle setup; the strongest signal in this run has been batch-size tuning around 160 rather than larger model changes.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 176","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
